Task description states 'operational data for 15 flight routes'. Verifier.py calculates row count from len(df) after loading Route_Financials sheet. Created test data with 15 routes and verified profitability distribution.

Created synthetic airline_profitability.xlsx with data designed to have exactly 3 routes with negative operating margins (Routes 5, 12, 13) which would be marked as 'REVIEW'. This is at the minimum of the expected range.

This is a maximum threshold. The verifier.py does not enforce this but the metadata suggests unprofitable routes should be between 3-6. Created test data with 3 routes marked REVIEW which is within this range.

privileged_info_summary: The airline_route_analysis task operates on a synthetic dataset file 'airline_profitability.xlsx' containing two sheets: Fleet_Specs (reference data for aircraft specifications) and Route_Financials (operational data for 15 regional airline flight routes). The Route_Financials sheet has exactly 15 data rows, and based on the calculations defined in the task description, 3-6 of these routes are expected to be unprofitable (negative operating margin) and marked as 'REVIEW'. The data is entirely synthetic and was created for this educational task to teach Excel formulas, VLOOKUP functions, and financial analysis. The task requires students to complete financial calculations (ASM, RPM, load factor, revenue, costs, margins) and identify unprofitable routes.
